old_claim.note: Sickle Harvesting Tools provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped composite harvesting evidence consists of deliberately produced flint blades whose wear and hafting traces identify their cutting function.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract, Introduction, and use-wear results analyze five glossed flint blades and identify cereal-cutting wear plus hafting traces on some inserts.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Ohalo II evidence is a set of manufactured flint cutting inserts, some of which preserve traces of hafting in a composite implement.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Stone-tool production is materially constitutive of the surviving inserts, whereas the later generic sickle node cannot cause an artifact that predates it.

ontology_before: The earliest composite harvesting inserts depended on a later broad sickle tradition.
ontology_after: The Ohalo II node depends directly on production of its evidenced flint inserts.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: composite_sickle_blades now requires stone_tool_making and no longer depends on sickle_harvesting_tools.
would_reject_if: Evidence showed that the Ohalo II artifacts were unmodified natural flakes rather than deliberately produced stone tools.
short_reason: The direct evidence is manufactured flint, and it predates the later generic sickle node.
